title: Home Away From Homer

jokes.text: Oh boy! This is what sunny sunday afternoons are for.
jokes.setup: NPR announces pretentious programming about dying Peruvian banjo music
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine enthusiasm for highbrow content contrasts his usual character
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony - Homer being excited about NPR's most boring content fits his contrarian nature

jokes.text: It's just, uh... It's not that good.
jokes.setup: Expert banjologist is brought on to discuss dying Peruvian banjo music
jokes.punchline: The expert admits the music form isn't good, undermining the whole premise
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of NPR pretension - the expert's honest assessment deflates the whole highbrow premise with perfect economy

jokes.text: Prison overcrowding? I've got a solution for that.
jokes.setup: NPR mentions David Sedaris discussing prison overcrowding
jokes.punchline: Homer's eager response implies a violent solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits Homer's character - his immediate violent instinct contrasts with NPR's intellectual approach

jokes.text: Her films are so lyrical! The phone lines are gonna be jammed.
jokes.setup: NPR offers tickets to obscure art film by director 'Ladswill Klernt'
jokes.punchline: Marge's prediction of high demand is immediately proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Sets up the joke that follows - Marge's cultural optimism about art film demand

jokes.text: Holy crap! Someone actually calling!
jokes.setup: Marge predicts jammed phone lines for art film tickets
jokes.punchline: NPR host's shock at receiving even one call
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - the juxtaposition of expected demand vs. reality, with excellent economy of language

jokes.text: Free movie tickets? I feel like Roger Ebert or as kiss ass new partner.
jokes.setup: Homer wins movie tickets
jokes.punchline: His dig at film critics getting free screenings, with specific reference to Ebert's partner
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural reference joke about film critics, though somewhat clunky in delivery

jokes.text: She'll be fine here, under the watchful eye of grandma Shark Week.
jokes.setup: Marge worries about leaving Maggie without a sitter
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests TV programming as a babysitter, personifying Shark Week
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - personifying TV programming as a grandmotherly figure is both original and character-appropriate for Homer

jokes.text: TV Shark is not a babysitter!
jokes.setup: Homer's absurd suggestion of Shark Week as babysitter
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated correction, treating the absurd premise seriously
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response - Marge treating the absurd premise with appropriate concern

jokes.text: Howdy, Homer, you here for viewing my shampooing?
jokes.setup: Homer arrives at Ned's house
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic awkward phrasing and oversharing about personal hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders dialogue - awkward oversharing combined with his characteristic speech pattern

jokes.text: Ho, sorry, but I promise to rewind videotapes for the poor.
jokes.setup: Homer asks Ned to babysit
jokes.punchline: Ned's oddly specific charitable commitment as an excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd charitable activity that perfectly captures Ned's overly helpful nature and the era's technology

jokes.text: Why won't you love me?
jokes.setup: Homer quotes the Bible about loving thy neighbor
jokes.punchline: His desperate, needy plea after attempting manipulation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's childish neediness after failed biblical manipulation shows his emotional immaturity

jokes.text: Hey, you wore a bathing suit in the bathtub? - Yeah, so I can't see my own shrinky dinck. Make sense.
jokes.setup: Bart questions Homer's bathing attire
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation about body shame and Bart's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor - the casualness of both the admission and acceptance makes it funnier

jokes.text: Mum, am I a 'butch' or 'femme'?
jokes.setup: Family is at an art theater with alternative newspapers
jokes.punchline: Bart innocently using LGBTQ+ terminology from personal ads
jokes.explanation: Decent fish-out-of-water humor with Bart encountering adult concepts, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: Honey, you can be anything you wanna be.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about LGBTQ+ terminology
jokes.punchline: Marge's supportive but oblivious response missing the context
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor - Marge's well-meaning but clueless supportiveness

jokes.text: Would you care for a segment? - Yes.
jokes.setup: Someone eating an apple during the movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate acceptance of offered food from a stranger
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - his food obsession overrides social norms about taking food from strangers

jokes.text: Mom, I don't wanna read, it's the weekend.
jokes.setup: Albanian film with subtitles
jokes.punchline: Bart's lazy refusal to read subtitles with weekend excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - treating subtitle reading like homework and using weekend as excuse

jokes.text: Oh, I'm stuck. Why did I eat that apple segment?
jokes.setup: Homer ate apple segment from stranger earlier
jokes.punchline: The apple segment somehow trapped him in his seat
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates consequences for Homer's earlier poor decision, though the logic is absurd

jokes.text: It's one of my humble figurines, he's scampered out of Parade magazine and into my nick neck nook and the... well his little buddies soon followed.
jokes.setup: Ned showing Maggie his collectibles
jokes.punchline: His awkward alliteration and overly precious description of mass-market collectibles
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned characterization - his precious language and treating cheap collectibles like fine art

jokes.text: You like 'em, huh? Come on, I'll show you the other 300.
jokes.setup: Ned showing off one figurine
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he has 300 more collectibles
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke - the scale of Ned's collection revealed efficiently, showing his obsessive nature

jokes.text: I feel so much empathy for those villagers. They had to drink their own tears.
jokes.setup: Family discussing the art film
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest emotional response to pretentious film content
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's sincere engagement with artistic pretension

jokes.text: Look at me, I'm a grad student, I'm 30 years old and I made 600$ last year!
jokes.setup: Bart making fun of people at the art theater
jokes.punchline: His specific impression of an impoverished grad student
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about grad student poverty with specific details that make it ring true

jokes.text: Bart, don't make fun of grad students. They just made a terrible life choice.
jokes.setup: Marge defending grad students
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded sympathy that's actually worse than mockery
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Marge appears to defend grad students but delivers an even harsher judgment with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Judas bear, you're not touching your last supper.
jokes.setup: Ned talking to his children at dinner
jokes.punchline: His religious-themed wordplay combining teddy bear with biblical betrayal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious wordplay that perfectly captures Ned's character - combining childish terms with biblical references

jokes.text: Well, it might be fun to be a landlord. Land fella! There's only one lord.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests Ned become a landlord
jokes.punchline: Ned's attempt at wordplay followed by religious correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - his awkward wordplay attempt immediately followed by religious self-correction

jokes.text: Wait, this is my license. - No, it's not, ya-hamp.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to pass as an adult renter
jokes.punchline: Ned discovers Bart is using someone else's ID, Bart's weak denial
jokes.explanation: Standard fake ID gag with Bart's characteristic weak lying attempt

jokes.text: Sometimes we saw all the boys in there... Robes.
jokes.setup: Foreign students explaining why they left co-ed dorms
jokes.punchline: The pause before 'robes' implies something sexual but it's actually innocent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - the pause and delivery suggests something inappropriate but reveals innocent modesty concerns

jokes.text: You poor thing. Now I've heard about those robes. Flapping everywhere...
jokes.setup: Foreign students' modesty concerns about seeing boys in robes
jokes.punchline: Ned's innocent empathy about the practical problems of loose robes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's complete innocence and genuine concern for practical modesty issues

jokes.text: Girls, welcome to your very own, Ned and breakfast.
jokes.setup: Ned welcoming the foreign students as renters
jokes.punchline: His play on 'bed and breakfast' inserting his own name
jokes.explanation: Typical Ned wordplay - not brilliant but fits his character's tendency toward awkward puns

jokes.text: You rhyme like Snoopy Dog. - Well, thank you.
jokes.setup: Ned's rhyming about catching Zs and earning degrees
jokes.punchline: Foreign student's mispronunciation of 'Snoop Dogg' and Ned's pleased acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference humor - the mispronunciation and Ned's oblivious pleasure at being compared to a rapper

jokes.text: I know what we can ask Jeeves. Why does he suck?
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa using internet search
jokes.punchline: Direct insult to search engine mascot
jokes.explanation: Period-specific tech humor that captures Bart's blunt attitude

jokes.text: Hey, a banner ad! - Sexyslumberparty.com? - It's flashing! We better click it!
jokes.setup: Kids using computer, encounter internet advertising
jokes.punchline: Their innocent response to obvious adult website advertising
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate humor about early internet advertising and children's naive responses

jokes.text: Hey, this is one of those dirty web sites. - Two girls? Who would want that?
jokes.setup: Kids discover adult website featuring two women
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent confusion about the appeal, ironically dismissing a common male fantasy
jokes.explanation: Great ironic humor - Lisa's innocent dismissal of something that's actually highly sought after by the target demographic

jokes.text: That's odd. I could sworn I just heard someone shriek my name.
jokes.setup: Homer's shocked reaction to seeing Ned's tenants on the website
jokes.punchline: Ned's oblivious response to Homer's off-screen reaction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for ongoing tension - Ned's innocent confusion while chaos erupts around him

jokes.text: And if you say Jimmy Fallon, I'll know you're lying.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what the kids are laughing at
jokes.punchline: His preemptive dismissal of a contemporary comedy figure
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about comedy itself and Homer's awareness of forced/fake laughter

jokes.text: Wait till I tell everyone about this, I'll feel important without drinking!
jokes.setup: Homer discovers scandalous situation with Ned's tenants
jokes.punchline: His excitement about gossip as alternative to alcohol for self-esteem
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Homer's awareness of his need for validation and usual means of achieving it

jokes.text: That will be weird.
jokes.setup: Homer's plan to feel important without drinking
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact observation about Homer's unusual sobriety
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Bart's casual acknowledgment of his father's drinking problem

jokes.text: Yep, and nobody's telling him. Not even his good buddy God.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ned being unaware of the situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual reference to God abandoning Ned
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about divine abandonment delivered with Homer's characteristic casual cruelty toward Ned

jokes.text: It's my nickname, Stupid_Flanders!
jokes.setup: Homer's extended rant about 'stupid Flanders'
jokes.punchline: His proud revelation that it's become his online identity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Homer's obsession with Ned - making it his actual online identity shows commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Hey, Moses, are you a loser? 'Yes I am, Homer.'
jokes.setup: Homer apparently talking to biblical figure
jokes.punchline: Moses allegedly agreeing with Homer's assessment
jokes.explanation: Surreal humor with Homer having casual conversations with religious figures who validate his opinions

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. They're just what my laptop needed.
jokes.setup: Mel asks about Ned's boarders
jokes.punchline: Homer's suggestive comment about his laptop 'needing' the webcam performers
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual double entendre that fits Homer's character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Son, I need to pee, but I can't stop watching. Fetch me a bottle.
jokes.setup: Homer addicted to watching webcam
jokes.punchline: His request for Bart to bring a bottle so he doesn't have to leave
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor showing Homer's complete lack of boundaries with his children

jokes.text: Come on dad, I've seen you hit the toilet from here.
jokes.setup: Homer's request for a bottle to urinate in
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact response about Homer's poor bathroom aim
jokes.explanation: Great gross-out humor with Bart's casual acceptance of his father's disgusting habits

jokes.text: We're watching the latest photos from Mars. - Ho-ho, great stuff. Worth every billion.
jokes.setup: Marge asks what they're watching
jokes.punchline: Bart's cover story and Homer's enthusiastic agreement about space exploration
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer's sudden enthusiasm for space exploration as a cover for pornography

jokes.text: Well, that's obvious. He doesn't even know.
jokes.setup: Marge wondering why Ned would allow the webcam situation
jokes.punchline: Her immediate understanding of Ned's innocence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's understanding of Ned's naivety

jokes.text: I'm thinking about getting a yogurt franchise. It's called Plops...
jokes.setup: Homer supposedly has something important to tell Ned
jokes.punchline: His complete deflection into discussing a poorly-named business venture
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven cowardice - Homer's inability to have difficult conversations leads to random business talk

jokes.text: Homer's the one what adviced us up to this sexy going's on.
jokes.setup: Ned discovers his tenants' webcam business
jokes.punchline: Foreign student's broken English revelation that Homer knew all along
jokes.explanation: Good plot escalation with the broken English making the revelation both funny and devastating for Ned

jokes.text: The Bible says: 'Cast your bread upon the waters.' But all I got was a bunch of soggy bread.
jokes.setup: Ned expressing disappointment in his friendship
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of biblical metaphor about kindness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor - Ned's literal biblical interpretation creates perfect metaphor for his disappointment

jokes.text: ...bread.
jokes.setup: Ned's emotional metaphor about soggy bread
jokes.punchline: Homer's inability to resist completing the word despite the serious moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his childish inability to resist the word repetition despite the emotional context

jokes.text: Today I realized they are more like the Midianites.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his disappointment to his sons
jokes.punchline: Obscure biblical reference for neighbor classification
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned characterization - using biblical taxonomy to classify modern relationships

jokes.text: I wish we lived in a place more like the America of yesteryear. that only exists in the brains of us Republicans.
jokes.setup: Ned wanting to move somewhere better
jokes.punchline: His self-aware acknowledgment of conservative nostalgia as fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire - Ned's moment of self-awareness about conservative mythology is both funny and insightful

jokes.text: I don't usually make big life decisions based on things stamped on things that I bought at the garage sale. But corn doggett, boys, it's time for a change!
jokes.setup: Ned looking at 'Made in Humbleton, PA' stamp
jokes.punchline: His acknowledgment of poor decision-making followed by doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor - Ned recognizes his poor reasoning while immediately following through with it

jokes.text: You're right. But you know who the real victim is here? Ned. - That's we've been trying to tell you!
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out Homer humiliated his best friend
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious agreement while completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - agreeing with the conclusion while missing that he's the cause

jokes.text: You're right. But you know who the real victim is here? - Ned. - That's we've been trying to tell you! - Ooh yeah...
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out Homer's role in hurting Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer's misdirected victimization followed by delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's self-centeredness followed by slow comprehension

jokes.text: Last-diddly-ast?
jokes.setup: Ned's emotional goodbye with his characteristic speech pattern
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused reaction to Ned's made-up word construction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Ned's characteristic speech pattern - Homer's confusion at the awkward construction

jokes.text: Well at least your name's on the bowl.
jokes.setup: Homer finds the new neighbor situation humiliating
jokes.punchline: Marge's attempt at consolation by pointing out his dog bowl has his name
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's degraded status - Marge's deadpan consolation actually highlights how bad things are

jokes.text: So, is that your whistle? - Damn straight. Clay Roberts. I coach wrestling, so everybody calls me coach...
jokes.setup: Homer meeting new neighbor
jokes.punchline: Aggressive neighbor's intimidating introduction
jokes.explanation: Sets up contrast with Ned effectively but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Listen neighborino, I wanna start our relationship all from a right foot so if you need anything, anything at all... - What jagoff left this here?
jokes.setup: Homer's attempt at friendly neighbor introduction
jokes.punchline: Clay's immediate hostility about Homer's own belongings
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff showing the contrast between Homer's hopeful politeness and Clay's immediate aggression

jokes.text: We've played this game called 'who can punch the softest'. And he always let me win.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be buddies with Coach Clay
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding of being physically abused as a game where he 'wins'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Homer's complete misunderstanding of abuse as friendly competition shows his desperate need for friendship

jokes.text: Howdy, Humbletonian! - Picnics and pixie-sticks!
jokes.setup: Ned arriving in wholesome small town
jokes.punchline: Greeting exchange that's aggressively wholesome to the point of absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good satire of small-town wholesomeness taken to ridiculous extremes

jokes.text: This town is sweeter than a cake made of pie.
jokes.setup: Ned expressing happiness with the wholesome town
jokes.punchline: His attempt at folksy metaphor that doesn't quite work logically
jokes.explanation: Typical Ned awkwardness - his attempt at colorful language creates a confusing but endearing metaphor

jokes.text: Even the dogs curb themselves.
jokes.setup: Ned praising the town's qualities
jokes.punchline: Noting even animals follow social conventions
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about excessive civic politeness

jokes.text: Get rid of dr. Fuzzenstein? I couldn't dream of thinking about considering...
jokes.setup: Ned told to shave his mustache for job
jokes.punchline: His dramatic name for his mustache and overwrought refusal
jokes.explanation: Great character humor - Ned's emotional attachment to his mustache expressed through naming it and verbose refusal

jokes.text: Daddy, why did you have to shave your nose neighbor?
jokes.setup: Ned forced to wear mustache cover
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent misphrasing
jokes.explanation: Cute child malapropism that adds to the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: My mustache has the right to life. It's my body and my choice!
jokes.setup: Pressure to conform to town standards
jokes.punchline: Conservative Ned using liberal reproductive rights rhetoric
jokes.explanation: Exceptional political satire - Ned inadvertently using pro-choice language while maintaining character consistency. Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: My mustache has the right to life. It's my body and my choice!
jokes.setup: Ned defending his mustache
jokes.punchline: Conservative character using pro-choice rhetoric to defend facial hair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political irony - Ned unwittingly using progressive talking points while maintaining his conservative character

jokes.text: Why would you do that? How am I gonna get home?
jokes.setup: Celebrity's car being destroyed on TV show
jokes.punchline: His practical concern about transportation after the spectacle
jokes.explanation: Decent reality TV satire with the celebrity's practical concern amid manufactured drama

jokes.text: Yeah, something tells me you won't starve, ha? Ha, chief? Ha?
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about losing power and not being able to eat without TV
jokes.punchline: Clay's fat-shaming with awkward attempt at camaraderie
jokes.explanation: Standard bully humor with Clay's aggressive fat jokes and awkward social cues

jokes.text: Like a woman whose made the biggest mistake of her life!
jokes.setup: Clay mocking Homer for reminiscing 'like a woman'
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific elaboration on the type of woman he's like
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's awareness of his poor decisions while accepting the gendered insult

jokes.text: Who am I fooling with my awesome lies?
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate story about randomly finding Ned
jokes.punchline: His proud self-assessment of his deception skills
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment - his pride in his obvious lies shows his complete lack of self-awareness about his transparency

jokes.text: Oh Ned, I don't deserve you as a neighbor, but will you forgive a young handsome fool and come home?
jokes.setup: Homer's sincere apology to Ned
jokes.punchline: His inability to resist describing himself as 'young handsome' during the apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - even during sincere apology, his vanity emerges with self-flattering description

jokes.text: That tears it. Boys, put on your goody two shoes! We're moving back to Springfield! But I have a girlfriend! - Now you got a pen pal.
jokes.setup: Ned deciding to leave after seeing the town's confusing mailbox
jokes.punchline: His dismissal of Rod's girlfriend concerns with mail-based relationship
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with practical father dismissing child's romantic concerns in characteristic Ned fashion

jokes.text: Yeah, I am a little fatty. You got me.
jokes.setup: Clay calling Homer 'fatso'
jokes.punchline: Homer's defeated acceptance of insult
jokes.explanation: Sad character moment showing Homer's broken spirit under abuse

jokes.text: Yeah, I am a little fatty. You got me.
jokes.setup: Clay calling Homer 'fatso' in front of others
jokes.punchline: Homer's meek acceptance and agreement with the insult
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor showing Homer's complete submission to his bully neighbor

jokes.text: I saw my advantage and I took it. That's what hero's do.
jokes.setup: Coach asking why Ned continued wrestling after he gave up
jokes.punchline: Ned's uncharacteristic ruthless behavior justified with heroic language
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - Ned's brief moment of unsportsmanlike behavior treated as heroism

jokes.text: Homer, did you steal the organ from the church? Maybe, we don't know that for sure.
jokes.setup: Church organ at Ned's welcome party
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-denial denial about obvious theft
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer criminal behavior with his characteristic non-denial approach to obvious guilt

jokes.text: Mind if I waggle my finger a little bit? - Go nuts!
jokes.setup: Ned enjoying looking down at his neighbors again
jokes.punchline: His request to add finger wagging gesture and Homer's enthusiastic permission
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Ned's polite request for permission to be judgmental

tmdb_overview: Flanders rents a room to two college girls who, without his knowledge, use the spare room to broadcast live softcore pornographic web videos of themselves.
